channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AI Phone Monitoring Shows Promise in Reducing Hospitalizations for Heart Failure Patients
item.description: A study led by cardiologist Aleix Olivella at Vall d’Hebron University Hospital explores the use of conversational artificial intelligence to remotely monitor patients with heart failure, a chronic an...

item.title: President Ruto Commits to Peaceful 2027 Elections and Respect for Results
item.description: President William Ruto has assured Kenyans that he will accept the outcome of the 2027 General Election, whether he wins a second term or not, attributing his political future to God’s plan. Speaking ...

item.title: Canadian Chamber of Commerce calls for stronger focus on competitiveness and economic growth ahead of federal budget
item.description: The Canadian Chamber of Commerce is urging the federal government to shift its attention away from ongoing concerns over U.S. trade measures, including tariffs associated with former U.S. president Do...

item.title: Judge allows Florida teen accused in cruise ship killing to remain with family pending detention decision
item.description: A U.S. federal judge has decided not to immediately detain a 16-year-old Florida boy accused of sexually assaulting and killing his 18-year-old stepsister, Anna Kepner, aboard a Carnival cruise ship i...

item.title: Opinion piece criticizes proposed Canadian surveillance powers in Bill C-22
item.description: This opinion article argues that the Canadian Liberal government poses a greater threat to civil liberties than large technology companies because of proposed surveillance legislation contained in Bil...

item.title: Tragic Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire leaves deaths as sponsor Ndungu Nyoro confirms safety of two students
item.description: A tragic dormitory fire broke out at Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil, Nakuru County, in the early hours of Thursday, May 28 at around 2 am, leaving the school community and the nation in shock. Repor...

item.title: Thomas Msengana appointed as interim host of Kaya 959 weekday breakfast show after Sizwe Dhlomo exit
item.description: Kaya 959 has confirmed that veteran broadcaster Thomas Msengana will take over the station’s weekday breakfast slot during June 2026, following the departure of well-known radio personality Sizwe Dhlo...

item.title: Twelve-year-old cyclist seriously injured in Brisbane collision with car
item.description: A 12-year-old boy is in hospital with serious injuries after a collision between his bicycle and a car in Brisbane’s north during Thursday afternoon rush hour. The crash occurred on Riordan Street in ...

item.title: Tanzania Home Affairs Ministry Requests Increased Budget Following Overspending and Security Operations
item.description: The Ministry of Home Affairs in Tanzania has requested a significant budget increase for the 2026/27 financial year, seeking TSh 2.4 trillion compared to the previous allocation of TSh 2.06 trillion, ...

item.title: Wadada Calls for Unity and Appeals to Ex-IGP Adamu to Rejoin APC in Nasarawa
item.description: Ahmed Aliyu Wadada, the APC governorship candidate for the 2027 election in Nasarawa State, has made a strong appeal for peace, unity, and reconciliation within the All Progressives Congress (APC). Du...

item.title: Late-April Freeze Causes Major Crop Losses Across Northeast Orchards
item.description: A rare late-April freeze has caused severe agricultural damage across the Northeastern United States, particularly in New Jersey and surrounding states, wiping out significant portions of apple and pe...

item.title: Driver throws passenger bus into Jhelum River after Rs5,000 traffic fine in Muzaffarabad
item.description: A shocking incident took place in Muzaffarabad where a passenger coach driver, reportedly angered after being issued a Rs5,000 traffic challan for violation of traffic rules, deliberately drove his bu...

item.title: Why continuing to use X is being questioned amid concerns over misinformation and algorithmic amplification
item.description: In this opinion piece, Jonathan Liew reflects on the state of Elon Musk’s social media platform X (formerly Twitter), arguing that it has become increasingly dominated by misinformation, algorithmic m...

item.title: AFP monitors 36 Chinese vessels in key West Philippine Sea areas
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported that 36 Chinese vessels were observed in critical areas of the West Philippine Sea this week, an increase from 27 last week. The vessels were tracked...

item.title: Albanese highlights India’s economic rise and strengthens Australia-India partnership ahead of Modi visit
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has highlighted India’s rapid economic growth and reaffirmed the strengthening strategic partnership between Australia and India. Speaking in the Australian ...

item.title: Yanga and Simba in tight title race as relegation fight intensifies in Mainland Premier League
item.description: The Mainland Tanzania Premier League has reached a very decisive stage as teams enter a 16-day break before the final matches of the season. With only a few fixtures remaining, the competition is stil...

item.title: AfDB projects moderate Tanzania growth of 5.4% in 2026 despite geopolitical risks
item.description: The African Development Bank (AfDB) has projected that Tanzania’s economy will grow by 5.4 percent in 2026, despite rising geopolitical tensions that are expected to increase energy and import costs a...

item.title: Complaint filed against former DBM chief Pangandaman over alleged irregular PhilHealth fund transfer
item.description: Former Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Secretary Amenah Pangandaman is facing a supplemental complaint before the Office of the Ombudsman over her alleged involvement in the controversial tr...

item.title: Karachi sacrificial cattle market concludes after selling nearly all animals ahead of Eid ul Adha
item.description: The main sacrificial cattle market of Karachi, spread over 1,100 acres at the Northern Bypass, has officially concluded its operations after witnessing an exceptional level of business activity ahead ...

item.title: Tension rises between Andhra Pradesh and Odisha over census operations in disputed Kotia villages
item.description: Officials from Andhra Pradesh have alleged that their census teams are being obstructed by Odisha authorities while attempting to collect Census-2027 data in the disputed Kotia villages along the Andh...
